name: June Meyer's Authentic Hungarian Sausage (Kol
recipeIngredient: 10 pounds Coarse ground pork (butt or pork shoulder)
1/3 cup Imported mild Hungarian paprika (or generic substitute)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil.
2. Add peeled garlic cloves and simmer for 20 minutes.
3. Remove garlic cloves and mash them with some water.
4. Add the mashed garlic and its water to the remaining water and mix everything thoroughly.
5. Mix all ingredients well, keeping the meat mixture cool.
6. If stuffing into casings, let the sausages hang at least 20 degrees (refrigerated) for a day.
7. Smoke sausage according to your smoker instructions.
8. If not stuffing, form into patties, wrap in foil, and freeze.
To cook Hungarian sausages:
1. Place fresh links in a heavy frying pan with a cover, ensuring they are half an inch below the water line.
2. Cover and bring water to a slow boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer sausage until it starts to change color.
4. Turn the sausage over and add more water if necessary to prevent burning.
5. Continue cooking slowly until all water is absorbed, resulting in a rich red-brown color.
6. Serve with sour cream and horseradish sauce, accompanied by potatoes and sauerkraut dishes.
Dried and smoked sausage can be used like pepperoni. For Italian sausage variations, omit paprika and use 2 ounces of whole fennel seed instead.
Please share your feedback if you try this recipe. Email June at june4@interaccess.com.
